column1: m3.3 As a User, I want to update my password (with OTP security).

column2: Verify that the user can update their password successfully
column4: 1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
column5:

2. Tap the "Change Password" option.
3. In the current password field, enter the current password
4. Tap on New Password input field, then enter a valid new password that meets the system’s password criteria.
5. Re-enter the same new password on the Confirm New Password input field.
6. Tap the "Submit" button.
column6:
1. The system sends a verification code (OTP) to the registered email address
2. The OTP is verified successfully upon input.
3. The password is updated.
4. The user can now log in using the new password.

column2: Verify that the user cannot request a new OTP upon password reset before the 1-minute cooldown period has passed
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the OTP screen after password reset flow.
column5: 1. Tap on the "Resend code" link.
column6:
1. "Resend code" should be disabled. 
2. No new OTP should be generated or sent.
3. The cooldown timer should continue until it completes the full 1-minute duration.

column2: Verify that the user can request a new OTP upon password reset
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the OTP screen after password reset flow.
column5:
1. Tap on the "Resend code" link.
2. Check the received email to confirm a new OTP has been sent.
3. On the OTP screen, enter the newly received OTP.
column6:
1. The system should display a prompt indicating that a new OTP has been sent to the registered email address.
2. The newly generated OTP should be received via email.
3. The user should be able to enter OTP.

column2: Verify if previously received OTP is not valid upon password reset
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the OTP screen after password reset flow.
column5:
1. Tap on the "Resend code" link.
2. Check the received email to confirm a new OTP has been sent.
3. Attempt to enter the previously received OTP.
4. Observe the system response.
5. Repeat steps 1-4.
column6:
1. The system should display a validation message: 

“OTP is either Invalid or has expired, Please try again or request a new OTP”

2. Every request of new OTP will trigger all previously requested OTP to be invalid.

column2: Verify validation message for incorrect OTP upon password update

column5:
1. Enter a registered email address in the prompt.
2. Tap “Submit” to proceed.
3. Check the provided email for the OTP code sent by the system.
4. Enter an invalid or expired OTP in the verification screen.
column6:

“OTP is either Invalid or has expired, Please try again or request a new OTP”

column2: Verify validation on the 5th failed OTP attempt (triggering max allowed attempts error)
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the  Profile page.
3.The system allows a maximum of 5 OTP attempts per session.
column5:
1. Tap on "Change Password"
2. Enter and submit the current password and new password
3. On the OTP screen, enter an invalid OTP for 4 times
4. On the 5th attempt, enter an invalid OTP again.
5. Observe the system behavior immediately after the 5th invalid entry.
6. Click" Ok"
column6:
1. Immediately after the 5th invalid OTP attempt, the system should display:
"Verification Failed: You have reached the maximum number of attempts. For your security, we’re redirecting you to the previous page."

2. The user should be redirected back to the password update screen.

column2: Verify validation on 5th OTP input attempt across multiple resend requests
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the OTP Verification page after initiating password update process.
3.The system allows a maximum of 5 OTP attempts per session.
column5:
1. Enter 2 invalid OTPs (Attempts 1 & 2).
2. Tap "Resend code".
3. Enter 2 more invalid OTPs (Attempts 3 & 4).
4. Tap "Resend code" again.
5. Enter one more invalid OTP (Attempt 5).
6. Observe the system behavior immediately after the 5th invalid entry.
7. Click" Ok"
column6:
1. Immediately after the 5th invalid OTP attempt, the system should display:

"Verification Failed: You have reached the maximum number of attempts. For your security, we’re redirecting you to the previous page."

2. The user should be redirected back to the password update screen.
column15: Teardown failure — UiAutomator2 accessibility timeout + Sign in locator not found

column2:
Verify validation when OTP session expires before max attempts 

(When the User has reached Session ID timeout without reaching the max input attempts.)
column4:
1. The mobile app is launched.
2. The user is on the OTP Verification page after initiating password update process.
3.The system allows a maximum of 5 OTP attempts per session.
column5:
1. Within 5 mins, input invalid OTP for 3 times
2. After 5 mins when the OTP session expires, regardless of validity input an OTP (4th attempt)
3. Tap on "Resend Code"
4. Tap on "Request New Code" button.
column6:
1. On the 4th OTP input attempt after session expiry, the system should display:

“OTP is either invalid or has expired. Please try again or request a new OTP.”

2. After tapping the "Resend Code" link, the system should show:

“OTP Expired: Your one-time password has expired. Request a new code to continue.”

3. After tapping the "Request New Code" button:

- The user should be redirected back to the password update screen.

- A new OTP session should be required to continue the password update process.

column2: Verify that the system displays the correct error modal when an invalid or valid OTP is entered after the 5-minute OTP validity window has expired.
column4:
1. User is on the OTP verification screen.
2. An OTP has already been generated.
3. The 5-minute OTP validity window has expired.
column5:
1. Wait until the OTP validity period exceeds 5 minutes.
2. Enter a valid or invalid OTP.
3. Observe the system behavior.
4. Tap the OK button on the modal.
column6:
1. System does not proceed with OTP verification.
2. An error modal is displayed with the message:
“OTP is either invalid or has expired. Please try again or request a new OTP.”
3. The modal displays an OK button.
4. Tapping OK redirects the user to the previous screen.

column2: Verify that the user's email is blocked for 60 minutes after 3 unverified Change Password OTP sessions with 5 invalid attempts per session
column4:
1.User is registered.
2. Email is not currently blocked.
column5:
1. Initiate Change Password Session #1
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)
2. Initiate Change Password Session #2
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)
3. Initiate Change Password Session #3
 - Enter 5 incorrect OTP(s)

4. Attempt to initiate Change Password Session #4
5. Click "OK"
column6:
1. After the 3rd unverified OTP session, if all 3 occurred within 15 minutes, the system blocks the user's email for 60 minutes.

2. After the 3rd unverified session, an error message is displayed in the Update Password page:
"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in 59 mins."

3. Any attempt in clicking "Change Password" from Profile page during the block period should return an error message:

"Change Password is currently unavailable. You can try again in 59 minutes."

4. Clicking "OK" will close the modal and user is still on the Profile page

5. Email is sent to the customer:
Subject: Security Notice: Unusual Activity Detected on Your Account

Body:
Hi <Customer Name>,

We noticed several attempts to access your account that exceeded our security limits. To help keep your account safe, we’ve temporarily locked it as a precaution.

If these attempts were made by you, no further action is needed. You can try signing in again after the cooldown period. If you need immediate access, please contact <replace customer support email> for assistance with resetting your account.

column2: Verify that the system does not block the user if the 3 unverified Change Password OTP sessions span more than 15 minutes
column4:
1.User is registered.
2. Email is not currently blocked.
3. Blocking occurs only after 3 unverified sessions within 10 minutes.
column5:
1. Start Session #1
 - Time: 00:00:00
 - Enter 2 incorrect OTPs then exit
 - Ends at: 00:05:00 min

2. Start  Session #2
 - Time: 00:05:00 min
 - Request OTP.
 - Enter 2 incorrect OTPs then exit
 - Ends at: 00:10:00 min

3. Wait for 5 mins
 - Time: 00:10:00 min
 - Ends at 00:15:00 min

4. Start Session #3
- Time: 00:16:00 min
- Enter incorrect OTPs then exit.
- Ends at: 00:20:00 mins

5. Start Session #4
 - Time: 00:20:00 or 00:21:00  min
 - Attempt to initiate send money session using the same email.
column6:
1. The system does not block the user.

2. The system evaluates the previous sessions:

 - Session #1: ended at 00:05

 - Session #3: started at 00:16 → more than 15 minutes since #1 ended

Therefore, the 3 unverified sessions do not fall within a 15-minute window.

3. Session #4 is allowed.

4. OTP is sent normally.

5. No blocking error is shown.

column2: Verify that the counter resets after successful verification of any change password OTP session
column4:
1. User is registered and not currently blocked.
2. Each OTP session allows a maximum of 5 attempts.
3. Session timeout is 5 minutes.
4. Blocking occurs only after 3 unverified sessions within 15 minutes.
5. Two previous send money sessions have already occurred, both unverified and/or expired.
column5:
1. Initiate change password Session #3
2. Enter 4 incorrect OTPs.
3. On the 5th attempt, enter the correct OTP.
4. Observe the outcome of the session.
5. Initiate change password Session #4
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification 

2.  Initiate change password Session #5
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification 

3.  Initiate change password Session #6
 - Leave the OTP input field blank
 - Cancel the OTP verification 

4. Attempt to initiate Change Password Session #7 using the same email."
column6:
1. Change Password OTP sessions 4-6 are counted as new failures.
2. No blocking should occur on session #6 because the previous two failures (1 & 2) were cleared due to the successful verification in #3.
3. Blocking would only occur if 3 new unverified sessions happen after a successful one - within a new 15-minute window so in this case on Session 7
4. On Session 7, a clear error message is displayed indicating the email is blocked:

"You have exceeded the maximum number of OTP attempts. You can try again in <time remaining> minutes."

column2: Verify validation message when user inputs invalid password format on New Password Field
column4:
1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
2. The user is on the “Update Password” screen.
column5:
1. Tap on New Password input field, then enter a new password that does not meet the system’s password criteria.
2. Tap the "Save" button.
column6:
1. In typing the new password, the system should display the validation message message below until the inputted password meets the system requirements: 

"Please follow the password complexity
         
1 Uppercase Letter
1 Lowercase Letter
1 Number
1 Special Character
8 Characters minimum
127 Characters maximum”

2. The "Submit" button should still be disabled.

column2: Verify the session timeout modal on the New Password set screen due to inactivity
column4: 1. The user is on the New Passwrord set page.
column5:
1. In the Profile screen, click the Change Password button and follow the flow.
2. In the New Password set screen, do not interact.
2. Wait 5 minutes.
column6:
1. Session Expired screen prompted with the "OK" button
2. Clicking the "OK" will redirect to the Login screen

column2: Verify the session timeout modal on the New Password set screen after minimizing
column4: 1. The user is on the New Passwrord set page.
column5:
1. Minimize app.
2. Wait 5 minutes.
3. Restore app.
column6:
1. Session Expired screen prompted with the "OK" button
2. Clicking the "OK" will redirect to the Login screen

column2: Verify the session timeout modal is still visible on New Password set screen after minimizing and reopening the app
column4: 1. The user is on the New Passwrord set page.
column5:
1. Do not interact.
2. Wait 5 minutes.
3. When the session timeout modal prompted, minimize the app.
4. Restore app.
column6:
1. Session Expired screen is still visible with the "OK" button
2. Clicking the "OK" will redirect to the Login screen

column2: Verify that session timeout resets on user activity and the homepage loads data normally.
column4: 1. The user is on the New Passwrord set page.
column5:
1. Remain on the page and occasionally tap the screen to indicate activity (scroll or tap every few seconds).
2. Continue this for at least 8 minutes.
3. Navigate back to homepage.
column6:
1. The session timeout timer should reset with every user activity.
2. Homepage, Transaction History, and Profile should load and display their data normally.

column1: m3.4 As a User, I want to log out of my account.

column2: Verify that the user can successfully log out from the mobile banking app
column4: 1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
column5:
1. Navigate to the profile section of the app.
2. Tap the "Logout" button.
3. On the confirmation prompt, tap "Yes" or "Confirm".
column6:
1. The system prompts the user  with a confirmation message:
"Are you sure you want to log out?" 

2. The user is successfully logged out.
3. The application redirects the user to the login screen.

column2: Verify that the user remains logged in when logout is cancelled
column4: 1. The user is logged into the mobile application.
column5:
1. Navigate to the profile or menu section of the app.
2. Tap the "Logout" button.
3. On the confirmation prompt, tap "No" or "Cancel".
column6:
1. The logout action is cancelled.
2. The user remains logged in and stays on the current screen.
column15: No confirmation modal — logout redirects directly to Sign In (feature behavior changed)

column1: m3.5 As a User, I want to request an account deletion.

column2: Verify that a user can access the Request Account Deletion option
column4:
1. The user is authenticated and logged into the mobile banking app.
2. The user has an active, verified account.
3. The user should not have any active loans/penalties.
4. The user should not have any active transactions with the account
5. All remaining balance must be withdrawn/transferred if possible
6. The user has cancelled Recurring Payments
column5:
1. Launch the mobile banking app.
2. Navigate to the profile menu.
3. Tap the “General Settings” button.
4. Tap the “Request Account Closure” button.
column6: 1. The user is redirected to the FAQs page showing the article on how to request account deletion.
column15: Feature not yet available — test case removed from automation suite
